## Overview
v2.9.15 delivers the first production Kubernetes signal layer in Community: managed node-pool baseline review for EKS, GKE, and AKS, integrated into the existing local-first scan and evidence workflow.

## Changes
- Added AWS rule `aws_eks_node_idle` and scanner path to detect low-utilization EKS-tagged nodes from EC2 + CloudWatch signals.
- Added GCP rule `gcp_gke_nodepool_review` and scanner path to inspect GKE cluster node-pool baseline/autoscaling configuration signals.
- Added Azure rule `azure_aks_nodepool_review` and scanner path to inspect AKS managed cluster node-pool baseline/autoscaling floor signals.
- Wired all three rules into Tauri scan execution flow with per-rule progress and diagnostics.
- Seeded new scan rule templates so account-level rule configuration surfaces Kubernetes rules by default for AWS/Azure/GCP.
- Updated docs FAQ and pricing scope text to reflect that initial managed Kubernetes node-pool signals are now available, while deeper allocation-level governance remains roadmap work.
- Bumped aligned product version to `2.9.15` across version-sync files, with `DOWNLOAD_LATEST_VERSION` intentionally unchanged at `2.9.13` (no new installer packaging in this rollout).

## Impact
- Community users can now see actionable Kubernetes baseline findings in the same report pipeline used for cloud resource waste.
- Teams get immediate visibility on managed cluster node-pool capacity posture before allocation-level integration lands.
- No installer artifact rollover was performed in this release; runtime download lane remains pinned to the prior packaged build.

## Upgrade/Download
- App metadata version: `2.9.15`
- Download artifact track: `2.9.13` (unchanged)
- Generic download entry: `https://cloud-waste-scanner.com/api/download/latest`
